standard: Tranh thủ kiếm con tối kho kìa.
A: Take advantage of the opportunity to catch some fish in the dark.
B: Take the opportunity to find a "tối kho" (dark-colored/deep-fried) one.
C: Take the opportunity to look for a night job tonight.

standard: Sao mà họ giàu thế nhỉ, tuyệt vời và ngưỡng mộ.
A: Wow, they're so rich! Amazing and admirable.
B: How are they so rich, it's wonderful and I admire them.
C: How are they so rich? Amazing and admirable.

standard: Sao mày nói tao ất ơ vậy mày.
A: Why are you calling me stupid?
B: Why do you call me so insignificant?
C: Why are you being so wishy-washy with me like that?

standard: Tầm này anh em có kèo là chơi hết mình.
A: At this point, if there's a bet, we'll go all out.
B: At this time, if we have a plan, we're going all out.
C: Around this time, we've got a game on and we're going all out.

standard: Có mấy cái cửa tận trong kia mà không vào được, cao điểm vào được không bạn?
A: There are a few gates further inside that are impassable; is it possible to get in during peak hours?
B: There are a few doors way back there that I can't get into; can I get in during peak hours?
C: There are a few doors way inside there that we can't get into. Can we get in during peak hours?

standard: Chồng con không lo kiếm đi, đi khắp nơi làm khổ bà con vậy bão ơi là bão.
A: Instead of focusing on earning a living, he goes around causing trouble for his neighbors, oh what a terrible storm!
B: My husband doesn't worry about earning a living, but instead goes everywhere causing misery for people; oh storm, oh storm.
C: Husband and kids don't bother making money, they go around everywhere causing trouble for others, what a mess!

standard: chứ sắp Tết rồi mà bảo không gần.
A: It's almost Tet (Vietnamese New Year), and you're saying it's not close?
B: But it's almost Tet, and you're saying it's not close.
C: But it's almost Tet and you say it's not close?

standard: TÌm cho ra con xe máy nhé chúng mày.
A: Find that motorbike, guys.
B: Find that motorbike, you guys.
C: Find that motorbike for me, you guys.

standard: mệt nhé! Không lẽ mua cái rộng rồi nhờ ông ấy qua gắn giúp
A: That's tiring! Should I buy a larger one and then ask him to come and install it for me?
B: So tiring! Maybe I should buy a larger one and then ask him to come over and help me install it.
C: I'm so tired! Do I really have to ask him to come over and install it after buying such a big one?

standard: Nghỉ 2 ngày không thấy chút bão đâu
A: We took two days off and saw no sign of a storm.
B: I've been off for two days and haven't seen any sign of the storm.
C: Took 2 days off and didn't see any storm at all

standard: Sợ hay sao mà không thấy nói gì.
A: Are they scared or something? They're not saying anything.
B: Are you scared or something that you aren't saying anything?
C: Scared or what, why aren't they saying anything?

standard: Biết ngay gì đâu cũng lên báo.
A: I knew it, but it ended up in the news.
B: I knew it, everything ends up in the news.
C: I knew it - anything and everything ends up on the news.

standard: Nóng vã hết mồ hôi, chẳng mưa chứ, chỉ chớp thôi.
A: It's so hot I'm sweating profusely, but it's not raining, just lightning.
B: It's so hot I'm drenched in sweat; there's no rain, only lightning.
C: So hot I'm drenched in sweat, not even raining, just lightning flashes.

standard: giờ không thấy luôn, mà ăn bột bột ngon nhỉ.
A: I can't find it anymore, but the floury texture was delicious, wasn't it?
B: I don't see it anymore, but the powdery stuff tastes delicious, doesn't it?
C: Now I can't find it at all, but eating bột is actually really delicious though.

standard: tao có mày rồi tao không lo đâu, chứ mà tao nghĩ tao cũng đảm đang chịu khó lắm chứ
A: I have you, so I don't have to worry. Besides, I think I'm quite capable and hardworking myself.
B: I have you, so I'm not worried; but I also think I'm quite capable and hardworking.
C: I have you now so I don't worry at all. But I think I'm also pretty capable and hardworking.

standard: đại gia tương lai đây chứ đâu nữa bạn nhỉ?
A: This is the future tycoon, isn't it?
B: This is a future tycoon right here, isn't it?
C: So this is the future tycoon right here, isn't it?

standard: gì chứ 3 triệu nắm chắc trong tay luôn
A: What? 3 million is definitely in my hands!
B: As for that, I've definitely got 3 million in my hand.
C: What are you talking about, 3 million is already firmly in my hands

standard: Lỡ viết cam kết 6-7 mà bây giờ còn 3 nữa
A: I accidentally wrote a commitment for 6-7 months, but now there are only 3 left.
B: I accidentally wrote a commitment for 6-7, but now there are only 3 left.
C: I made 6-7 commitments but now only have 3 left

standard: hôm nào chọn ngày đẹp đi tiếp nhé
A: Let's choose a nicer day to continue our trip.
B: Let's pick a good day to go out again sometime.
C: Let's pick a nice day and continue on, okay?

standard: Sao ảnh nó thất lạc đâu cả rồi, còn được ảnh này nữa
A: Where did all the other photos go? Only this one remains.
B: Why are all the photos lost, and only this one is left?
C: Why did all my photos of it go missing, at least I still have this photo too

standard: chín ngon lắm đấy, hôm nào có chín chị cho em ăn thử, còn sống thì nó đắng nghét à.
A: Ripe ones are delicious! When you have some ripe ones, please let me try some. The raw ones are really bitter.
B: It's very delicious when ripe; when it's ripe, I'll let you try some, but it's extremely bitter when it's raw.
C: When it's ripe, it's really delicious. When you have ripe ones, let me try some. But when it's unripe, it's extremely bitter.

standard: không thấy tiết mục kiểm tra bảo hiểm tai nạn dân sự nhỉ?
A: I didn't see any section about checking civil liability insurance.
B: I don't see a section for checking civil liability insurance, do I?
C: I don't see the segment for checking civil accident insurance, huh?

standard: làm như nhà bà ấy không bằng, bà ấy rành khủng khiếp
A: It's as if she doesn't know anything about her house, she's incredibly knowledgeable.
B: It's as if it were her own house; she knows it incredibly well.
C: I can't do it as well as her family does, she knows it incredibly well

standard: làm tôi muốn khui bia thật nhé
A: It makes me want to open a beer!
B: It really makes me want to open a beer.
C: So I really want to crack open a beer, okay?

standard: Nhưng trâu ăn lúa bị phạt tiền liền
A: But buffaloes that eat rice are immediately fined.
B: But if the buffalo eats the rice, there's an immediate fine.
C: But buffalo that eat rice are fined immediately

standard: vậy đi mua chắc đem cái thau theo hay lấy túi nhựa mang về
A: So when I go shopping, should I bring a basin or take a plastic bag home?
B: So when going to buy it, should I bring a basin or use a plastic bag to carry it back?
C: So when going shopping, should I bring the basin along or just use plastic bags to bring things home?

standard: Năm này ngoài Đông Hà lúa được mùa không?
A: Besides Dong Ha, is the rice harvest good this year?
B: Is the rice crop successful outside of Dong Ha this year?
C: Is the rice harvest good this year in Đông Hà?

standard: Cô Hằng Nguyễn có tiền đâu mà rủ đi ăn.
A: Ms. Hang Nguyen doesn't have any money to invite anyone out to eat.
B: Ms. Hang Nguyen doesn't have the money to invite anyone out to eat.
C: Ms. Hằng Nguyễn doesn't have money anyway, so why would you invite her to go eat.

standard: 2 con báo đánh rồi chọc nhau cả ngày chứ không phải thiên thần.
A: Those two leopards fought and teased each other all day, they weren't angels.
B: Two little troublemakers who fight and tease each other all day, not angels.
C: 2 little leopards who've already been fighting and teasing each other all day, not angels at all.

standard: Khó khắn gì cũng cố găng chứ.
A: No matter how difficult it is, we must try our best.
B: No matter the difficulties, I'll keep trying.
C: No matter what difficulties, I'll keep trying anyway.

standard: Sắp về rồi bạn , một tháng rưỡi nữa tôi về
A: I'm coming home soon, in a month and a half.
B: I'm coming back soon, I'll be back in another month and a half.
C: I'm about to come back, buddy. In a month and a half, I'll be back.

standard: Đang lo cho vợ con, mà nghĩ tận Cần Thơ như nào làm gì
A: I'm worried about my wife and children, why would I be thinking about what's happening all the way in Can Tho?
B: I'm worried about my wife and children, but thinking about how to get all the way to Can Tho and what to do there.
C: I'm worried about my wife and kids, so why am I even thinking about Cần Thơ anyway

standard: Ăn tối, uống một lon bia xong không dám ra đường.
A: After having dinner and a can of beer, I didn't dare go out.
B: Had dinner, drank a can of beer, and now I don't dare go out on the street.
C: I had dinner, drank a can of beer, and now don't dare go outside.

standard: chắc không sao đâu nhỉ. Không xảy ra án mạng gì chứ?
A: It should be alright, right? No murders, right?
B: It'll probably be fine, right? No murders happened, did they?
C: It should be fine, right? No murder happened, right?

standard: Nghèo đâu không biết, đi dọc đường toàn ô tô.
A: I don't know where the poverty is, but all I see along the road are cars.
B: I don't know about being poor, but all I see along the road are cars.
C: What poverty? I wouldn't even know. Walking down the street, all I see are cars.

standard: về luôn chứ, mua vé giờ còn kịp đấy chị, về đi Khe Sanh nào.
A: Let's go now, you can still buy tickets, let's go to Khe Sanh!
B: Of course, come back now; there's still time to buy tickets, sister, let's go back to Khe Sanh.
C: Let's go right away, buying tickets now is still on time, sis, let's go to Khe Sanh.

standard: Bữa sơ thèm, giờ không thèm nữa.
A: I was craving it the first time, but I don't anymore.
B: I craved it before, but I don't crave it anymore.
C: I was craving it earlier, but now I don't want it anymore.

standard: Không thấy "đầu con trâu" nhỉ?
A: I don't see the "buffalo head" anywhere, do I?
B: I don't see the "buffalo head," do I?
C: Can't see the "buffalo head" though?

standard: Con ai mà thấy quen nhỉ?
A: Whose child is that? It looks familiar.
B: Who is this kid? They look familiar.
C: Whose kid is this? Seems familiar.

standard: hốt lúa mệt khủng khiếp luôn Tí ơi Tí.
A: Harvesting rice is incredibly tiring, Tí!
B: Harvesting rice is incredibly exhausting, Ti oh Ti.
C: I'm so incredibly exhausted from harvesting rice, oh my gosh.

standard: Nó lạnh cha lạnh ông vậy nhỉ?
A: It's so cold, isn't it?
B: Why is it so freezing cold?
C: It's this cold? Damn!

standard: hồi đấy còn bà ngoại hay nướng ăn như vầy này chị, lúc mà nhà ngoại còn nguyên một vườn đào ấy.
A: Back then, my grandmother used to grill peaches like this, when her family owned a whole peach orchard.
B: Back then, my maternal grandmother used to grill and eat them like this, back when her house still had a whole garden of peaches.
C: Back then, grandmother used to roast things like this for us to eat, sis, back when the grandparents' house still had an entire peach garden.

standard: nhìn thèm khủng khiếp luôn bày đặt giả vờ xấu hổ nữa.
A: It looks incredibly tempting, and yet they're pretending to be embarrassed.
B: It looks incredibly tempting, and yet they're pretending to be shy.
C: Looking at that with such intense longing, and then pretending to be embarrassed again.

standard: thật chứ, gì cũng có thể làm được, sợ thật
A: Seriously, anything is possible, it's terrifying.
B: Seriously, anything can be done, it's really scary.
C: Seriously, anything is possible, it's really scary

standard: Tiền đâu cho lại em nhỉ?
A: Where would I get the money to give you back?
B: Where is the money to give back to me?
C: Where should I get the money to pay you back?

standard: Vậy xa đâu thì cũng chúc sinh nhật được một câu chứ, vậy bảo sao không vui
A: No matter how far away you are, you can still say happy birthday, right? That's why I'm so happy.
B: So even if it's far, I could still wish you a happy birthday, so why say you're not happy?
C: So no matter how far apart, you can still wish happy birthday with a message, so how can you not be happy?

standard: Vô Kỳ Anh lần đầu ăn
A: Vo Ky Anh ate for the first time.
B: Vo Ky Anh eating for the first time
C: First time eating at Vo Ky Anh

standard: ơn trời, sắp không phải lụy đò rồi ông ơi.
A: Thank goodness, we won't have to rely on the ferry anymore, sir.
B: Thank god, I'm almost done being obsessed with that person, man.
C: Thank goodness, I won't have to be dependent on the ferry anymore, man.

standard: đồn bậy thật chứ, một đứa có nửa thùng chứ nhiêu mà đồn tửu lượng cao
A: That's a really bad rumor. One person only has half a case of beer, so why spread the rumor that they have a high alcohol tolerance?
B: The rumors are really ridiculous; someone who can only drink half a bucket is being rumored to have a high alcohol tolerance.
C: That's a complete lie, someone who can only drink half a crate, yet they spread rumors about having a high alcohol tolerance

standard: Ủa con này hồi chiều mày xem này
A: Hey, look at this one, you saw it this afternoon!
B: Wait, you looked at this one this afternoon.
C: Hey, this kid - you saw this earlier this afternoon, right?

standard: Thế này mà không ngon nữa thì thôi.
A: If this isn't delicious, then I give up.
B: If it's not delicious even like this, then I give up.
C: If this isn't delicious anymore, then forget it.

standard: Đang bị đứt gãy tầng địa chất. Rất nguy hiểm.
A: The geological strata are currently experiencing faulting. This is very dangerous.
B: There is a geological fault. It is very dangerous.
C: The geological layer is fracturing. It's very dangerous.

standard: Rồi thêm câu " lại đây nói nghe này " làm gì vậy.
A: And then, why add the phrase "Come here and listen to this"?
B: Then why add the sentence "come here and tell me about it"?
C: Then why did you add "come here and listen to this"?

standard: đi vậy chứ hỏi Việt Nam ăn mấy trái có cô nào biết đâu. Thật buồn.
A: I went there, but when I asked how many Vietnamese fruits we eat, none of the girls knew. How sad.
B: Going like that, but if you ask how many fruits Vietnam eats, no lady would know. Truly sad.
C: Fine then, asking how many fruits Vietnam consumes, there's no girl who would know. So sad.

standard: Đừng nói vậy, bạn xấu hổ
A: Don't say that, you're embarrassed.
B: Don't say that, you're embarrassing yourself.
C: Don't say that, you'll be embarrassed

standard: Hồi xưa trên tôi bán 3 nghìn/cốc, đội bạn tôi thách tôi ăn được hẳn 3 cốc no căng bụng luôn.
A: Back in the day, they sold them for 3,000 VND per cup where I live. My friends challenged me to eat three cups and be completely full.
B: Back then, I sold them for 3 thousand per cup, and my friends challenged me to eat as many as 3 cups until I was completely full.
C: Back in the day around here, I sold them for 3,000 per cup, my group of friends challenged me to eat a full 3 cups until I was stuffed to bursting.

standard: Nhà ngoại không có gì để lấy nữa đâu cô.
A: There's nothing left for you to take from my mother's family, miss.
B: There is nothing left to take from my maternal grandparents' house, ma'am.
C: There's nothing left to take from my grandparents' house anymore, Aunt.

standard: Kìa, chúng mày xem lịch sao kìa, tôi lâu rồi không đi Vinh chơi.
A: Look at the schedule, guys! It's been a long time since I went to Vinh.
B: Look, you guys check the calendar, I haven't gone to Vinh for fun in a long time.
C: Look, you guys check the calendar, why is it that I haven't been to Vinh for fun in so long?

standard: À đấy. Nhìn giống chị Thơ.
A: Ah yes. She looks like Thơ.
B: Oh, that's it. Looks like Ms. Tho.
C: Ah, there. Looks like chị Thơ.

standard: Có chơi không anh? tôi vào
A: Are you going to play? I'm coming in.
B: Are you playing? I'm coming in.
C: Are you playing? I'm in.

standard: Mẹ tớ 95 tuổi vẫn trèo đánh đống rơm... Nể không?
A: My mom is 95 years old and still climbs to make haystacks... Amazing, right?
B: My mother is 95 years old and still climbs haystacks... Impressive, right?
C: My mom is 95 years old and still climbing haystacks... Impressive, right?

standard: dự báo mưa mà sao nay nắng đẹp quá trời. Kì thật chứ!
A: The forecast said it would rain, but it's so sunny today! How strange!
B: The forecast predicted rain, but why is it so sunny today? How strange!
C: They said it would rain, but it's so beautifully sunny today. How weird, right?

standard: Dân Bến Lức xấu hổ cho
A: The people of Ben Luc are ashamed.
B: Bến Lức people are ashamed of this.
C: The people of Bến Lức are so ashamed.

standard: Giá rẻ thế chẳng lẽ lại phải sang tận HL gội đầu ư
A: With such cheap prices, do I really need to go all the way to Holland to get my hair washed?
B: The price is so cheap, could it be that I have to go all the way to HL just to get my hair washed?
C: So cheap, yet I'd still have to go all the way to HL just to get my hair washed, huh

standard: Người Nghệ An không làm ta thất vọng. Chúc hai cháu hạnh phúc đến răng long mỏi gối nhé.
A: People from Nghe An never disappoint. Wishing you both a lifetime of happiness.
B: People from Nghe An do not let us down. I wish you two happiness until your hair turns grey and your knees grow weary.
C: Nghe An people never disappoint me. Wishing you two happiness until your teeth fall out and your knees grow tired, okay?

standard: Tầm này lừa mà cưới đi chứ.
A: At this point, you should just trick them into marrying you.
B: At this point, just trick them into marrying you.
C: At this point, just trick them into getting married already.

standard: 3 em giỏi lắm chứ gặp chị chắc sẽ "nhặt được của rơi đồng thời bỏ túi luôn".
A: Those three kids are so clever, but if they met me, they'd probably "find a lost treasure and pocket it at the same time."
B: You three are very clever, but if you had met me, you would have "picked up something dropped and pocketed it at the same time."
C: You 3 are really good, but if you meet me, you'll probably learn that I pick up what's dropped and pocket it right away.

standard: không biết cây dừa chịu nổi không đấy nhé !
A: I don't know if the coconut tree can withstand that!
B: I wonder if the coconut tree can withstand it!
C: I wonder if that coconut tree can even handle it!

standard: Đâu mà cực dữ vậy.
A: Why is it so incredibly difficult?
B: Why is it so exhausting?
C: It's not that intense at all.

standard: Sao mà đủ 40 cái?
A: How did you manage to get 40 of them?
B: How can there be enough for 40?
C: How could there be 40?

standard: Có cần đính chính lại câu không biết nhậu với không biết chửi chồng lại chút không.
A: Should I clarify the statements about not knowing how to drink alcohol and not knowing how to yell at my husband?
B: Do I need to correct the parts about not knowing how to drink and not knowing how to scold my husband a bit?
C: Do I need to clarify the sentence about not knowing how to drink and not knowing how to scold the husband a bit?

standard: không thấy em nào khóc lóc nhỉ?
A: I didn't see any of the kids crying, did I?
B: I don't see any girls crying, do I?
C: I don't see anyone crying, huh?

standard: bình thường như vậy mà nhổ về trồng được hết bạn ơi. Về nhổ bán tôi xem.
A: It's perfectly normal; you can pull them up and replant them, my friend. Pull them up and sell them to me so I can see.
B: Normally, they are just like that, but you can pull them up and plant them all, my friend. Pull some up and sell them to me to see.
C: Just like this, you can pull them up and replant them all, my friend. As for pulling them up to sell, let me check.

standard: Mệt lắm em à, tiền mà nhiều thì giờ không phải đi phá lung tung nữa đâu.
A: I'm so tired, honey. If I had more money, I wouldn't be wasting it like this anymore.
B: I'm so tired; if I had a lot of money, I wouldn't have to go around struggling like this.
C: I'm so tired, kid. If we had more money, we wouldn't have to go out and hustle like this anymore.

standard: Vẫn không thấy chủ nhân của 10 triệu bình luận gì hết em nhỉ?
A: Still haven't seen any comment from the owner of those 10 million comments, have you?
B: Still haven't seen the owner of the 10 million say anything, have we?
C: I still can't find the owner of 10 million comments at all, can I?

standard: Nuốt có được đâu mà bảo không trả
A: How can you say you won't pay when you can't swallow it?
B: I couldn't even swallow it, so why say I wouldn't pay?
C: You can't even keep it, yet you're saying you won't return it

standard: Nên ai ngứa đầu mau xuống tôi gội cho để lát tôi còn du Xuân
A: So, anyone with an itchy scalp, come down so I can wash your hair, so I can go out for the spring festivities later.
B: So whoever has an itchy head, come down quickly and I'll wash it for you so that I can go celebrate the Lunar New Year in a bit.
C: So anyone with an itchy scalp, come quickly and let me wash your hair for you, so later I can still go out and enjoy the Tet holiday.

standard: Chở 4 đứa chứ mấy nhỉ?
A: I'm only carrying four kids, right?
B: Carrying 4 people, right?
C: It can take 4 kids, but how many more?

standard: định nhậu lai rai vài chai chứ nói gì nữa nhỉ?
A: We just wanted to have a few drinks, what else would we talk about?
B: I'm planning to have a few beers and snack for a while, what else is there to say?
C: I was planning to hang out and have a few drinks, what else is there to say?

standard: muội chỉ phát hiện thiếu bát canh khổ qua nhồi thịt thôi
A: I only noticed that the bitter melon soup stuffed with meat is missing.
B: I only noticed that the stuffed bitter melon soup was missing.
C: I only found out that one bowl of bitter melon soup stuffed with meat is missing.

standard: Ngon thật, còn thiếu thứ gì nữa, nếu không tiêu chảy chết
A: It's delicious, what else could it be but to die of diarrhea?
B: It's really delicious, but what else is missing? Otherwise, I'll die of diarrhea.
C: Really delicious, what else is missing—diarrhea and death?

standard: đừng cúp nữa, sợ lắm rồi, cúp gì mà 6 giờ sáng tới 4 rưỡi chiều, nắng nóng khủng khiếp!
A: Please stop the power outage, I'm already scared! What kind of power outage is this, from 6 AM to 4:30 PM, in this terrible heat!
B: Stop the power cuts, I'm terrified already; what kind of power cut lasts from 6 AM until 4:30 PM, the heat is terrible!
C: Don't cut the power anymore, I'm so scared already. What kind of blackout lasts from 6 AM to 4:30 PM during this terrible heat!

standard: Già mẹ mày, à Thái Lê còn trẻ đấy mày.
A: Your mother's old, oh wait, Thai Le is still young, you know.
B: Your mother is old, oh, Thai Le is still young.
C: Your mom is old, damn, but Thái Lê is still young though, man.

standard: nói linh tinh nó bắt cổ bây giờ.
A: If you talk nonsense, they'll arrest you now.
B: Keep talking nonsense and they'll grab you by the neck.
C: I say random stuff and they grab me by the neck now.

standard: trông mưa lớn trôi thằng bốc phét kia cho rồi
A: I hope the heavy rain washes away that boastful guy.
B: I hope the heavy rain just washes that braggart away.
C: Hope the heavy rain washes that blowhard away already

standard: Sao không quay những cảnh rừng cây bẻ gãy đôi cho lên mạng?
A: Why not film scenes of trees being broken in half and post them online?
B: Why not film the scenes of trees being snapped in half and post them online?
C: Why not film scenes of forests with trees snapped in half and post them online?

standard: Nhà mình cũng có 6 cô con gái nhỉ?
A: My family also has six daughters, right?
B: Our family also has 6 daughters, right?
C: My family also has 6 daughters, right?

standard: Tôi nhìn thấy mắt bão mà khiếp. Chứ không nở hoa được nữa, thương.
A: I was terrified when I saw the eye of the storm. The flowers won't be able to bloom anymore, it's a pity.
B: I saw the eye of the storm and it was terrifying. Now it can't bloom anymore, how heartbreaking.
C: I saw the eye of the storm and was terrified. Now I can't bloom anymore, dear.

standard: ông ấy không được đi chơi nên ngày nào ông ấy cũng khóc.
A: He wasn't allowed to go out and play, so he cried every day.
B: He wasn't allowed to go out, so he cried every day.
C: He is not allowed to go out, so he cries every day.

standard: Trong nghĩa địa mọc đầy luôn, ăn chua chua ngọt ngọt.
A: They grow everywhere in the cemetery, and they taste sweet and sour.
B: They grow everywhere in the cemetery; they taste sour and sweet.
C: It grows all over the cemetery, tasting sour and sweet.

standard: Cái này có khi chú rể gọi mẹ vợ bằng em nhỉ?
A: Perhaps the groom calls his mother-in-law "em" (a term of endearment used for younger women) in this context?
B: In this case, maybe the groom calls the mother-in-law "em"?
C: I wonder if the groom might call his mother-in-law by "em"?

standard: Ngã ngang đấy chứ.
A: It fell sideways.
B: That's quite a fall.
C: Fell right there, honestly.

standard: Cả tỉnh nghe luôn chứ huyện ăn thua gì?
A: The whole province heard about it, so what does the district matter?
B: The whole province will hear about it, so what does the district even matter?
C: The whole province already heard about it, so what's a district gonna do about it?

standard: Túi bần như túi ổi nhỉ. Mô hình kinh tế kiểu mới
A: A woven bag, like a guava bag, huh? A new economic model.
B: Poor bags are like guava bags, aren't they. A new type of economic model.
C: A bag of bần is like a bag of guava, huh. The new economic model.

standard: Năm nay nghe đâu đặt chỗ rồng năm ngoái.
A: I heard they're reserving the dragon spot from last year this year.
B: I heard that this year's bookings are more crowded than last year's.
C: I hear this year's already booked, just like last year.

standard: mang cái ghe máy lên có vẻ ngon
A: Bringing the motorboat aboard looks promising.
B: Bringing the motorized boat up seems like a good idea.
C: bringing the motorboat up looks good

standard: 2 tỷ được rồi. Đòi tận 10 tỷ rồi khi nào mới về được
A: 2 billion is enough. They're demanding 10 billion, when will they ever get it back?
B: 2 billion is enough. Demanding as much as 10 billion, when will I ever be able to return?
C: 2 billion is already done. Asking for as much as 10 billion, so when will I ever get it back?

standard: Bánh ướt ram giò lắt. Ngon ơi là ngon.
A: Steamed rice rolls with sliced ​​pork sausage. Absolutely delicious.
B: Steamed rice rolls with fried pork sausage. So incredibly delicious.
C: Bánh ướt ram giò lắt. So delicious!

standard: Ở nhà đóng cửa tự chơi một mình có được không
A: Is it okay to stay home and play by myself with the door closed?
B: Is it okay to stay home, close the door, and play by myself?
C: Can I just stay home, close the door, and play alone?

standard: Ai bảo anh hùng anh bá rồi thành báo luôn.
A: Who said that heroes and tyrants turn into leopards?
B: Who said being a hero and being powerful leads to becoming a burden?
C: Who said the hero turned into a lord and became a reporter too.

standard: Qua vừa bảo mày đây này.
A: I just told you this.
B: I told you just yesterday.
C: I literally just told you right here.

standard: Chiều đi ngắm biển đỡ thèm chứ không tắm
A: We went to admire the sea in the afternoon to satisfy our craving for swimming, but we didn't go swimming.
B: I'll go look at the sea this afternoon to satisfy my craving, but I won't swim.
C: Went to see the sea in the afternoon to ease the longing, but didn't swim

standard: Tàn ác thật sự. Cho bà ấy đi tù là vừa rồi
A: That's truly cruel. She deserves to be sent to prison.
B: Truly cruel. Putting her in prison is only fitting.
C: That's truly cruel. She deserves prison time, plain and simple.
